# Instagram Post — Holotron-12B Achieves 80.5% on WebVoyager — Open-Source Computer-Use Agents Hit Inflection Point
# Week: Week of 2026-03-23
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-03-29T23:02:24.847Z

---

35% to 80%. That is how fast AI agents learned to use computers.

H Company and NVIDIA just released Holotron-12B — an open-source model with only 12 billion parameters that scores 80.5% on the WebVoyager benchmark for autonomous web navigation. The previous generation scored 35.1%.

In the same week, OpenAI's Codex subagents went live for everyone — parallel task execution in isolated sandboxes. And MCP, the protocol connecting AI agents to tools, crossed 97 million installs.

Here is what matters for business leaders: a model small enough to run on your own infrastructure can now navigate websites, complete multi-step tasks, and operate autonomously at scale. And it is open source. No vendor lock-in. No permission needed.

The question is not whether AI agents can handle knowledge work. It is which workflows you hand over first, how you govern them, and what happens when your competitors move faster.

GYanTech AI helps executive teams identify and deploy agentic automation with measurable ROI. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- H Company and NVIDIA released Holotron-12B, an open-source 12B parameter model for computer-use agents — Source: Upsun AI Weekly Review, 3/23/2026
- WebVoyager performance jumped from 35.1% to 80.5% — Source: Upsun AI Weekly Review, 3/23/2026
- OpenAI Codex subagents hit general availability, enabling parallel task execution in isolated sandboxes — Source: Upsun AI Weekly Review, 3/23/2026
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) crossed 97 million installs — Source: Digital Applied March 2026 AI Roundup
